mentions.note: Laid to waste on AI disruption narrative; market overestimating AI obsolescence risk.
Enterprise software weakness; stock really hammered; part of broader tech malaise.
Enterprise software suffering irrational multiple compression from AI platform fears; no actual earnings misses; money rotating to storage plays; wait for stabilization.
Down 7% as enterprise software cohort punished; reported good numbers but stock withering due to AI replacement concerns; P/E compression ongoing.
AI software threat; Anthropic potentially superior to Adobe's offering; enterprise software cohort under siege.
Enterprise software cohort under attack as Anthropic and competitors roll out AI alternatives; valuation multiples under pressure.
Up 3.9% as Anthropic invincibility narrative fades; enterprise software not easily replaced by AI code.
Enterprise software beneficiary of bounce; market overestimated AI threat; management teams actively protecting business.
Creative software facing existential threat from AI upstarts offering natural-language creative generation; mentioned as part of legacy software 'destruction.'

Design software down 30%+ Q1, down 65% from Nov 2021 peak, trades 10x earnings like homebuilder; snake-bitten former cloud king; every new design tool (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Figma, Canva) drives stock lower. Potential design school exodus existential. Can always get cheaper. Cramer won't say too cheap.
Software/creative tools under pressure; down 4% from hardware/AI rotation.
Big $7.5B contract win, but company making no money in a market where many competitors are profitable. Switching from losers to winners; not the time to own ADBE.
Software stock in disfavored market. Market rotation away from software.
Part of software rotation; up 4.5% today. Group rally may be met with selling after few days of strong performance.
Traditional enterprise software losing pricing power to AI; Anthropic/OpenAI can replicate capabilities cheaper; primacy and supremacy threatened by AI agents + hardware alternatives.
SaaS sector headwind: AI efficiency concerns pressure per-user revenue model.
Part of SaaS cohort that's shrinking. Competition from cheaper design software rising; even after thrashing down, Cramer thinks 'not low enough to own' — more downside likely coming.

CFO quit for Marvell Tech, CEO resigned; quarter disappointing with recurring revenue slowdown; valued at $82B but worth ~$60B max; in secular decline; maybe bounces to $220 but fundamentals broken; get out.
CFO departed for Marvell, CEO already resigned; disappointing quarter; recurring revenue slowdown; secular decline; avoid even on 42% pullback.

Strong Q1: 13% revenue growth, 18% earnings growth; beat guidance; FY raised 12% sales, 16-17% earnings. Firefly ARR tripled, creative premium users 50M→90M. But CFO Durn leaving for Marvell and CEO stepping down create paralyzing leadership void. Stock down 70% from 2021, trades 8.5x earnings despite double-digit growth. Enterprise software hated. Wait for next CEO and CFO before betting on comeback.
